zh.lifeSummary: 當Olivier II de Clisson於1236年出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易洛易-阿特藍可可利頌時，他的父親Olivier Ier de Clisson31歲，母親Plaisou de la Roche-Derrien34歲。他於大約1263年與Jeanne Marie Bertrand de Bricquebec結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1298年享年62歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clisson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Clas, a shortened form of Niklas (see Nicholas ). In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Klasson. Compare Clason 1.

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Clason
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto.
Americanized form (and a less common Swedish variant) of Swedish Classon or Klasson.
Americanized form of German, Danish, Norwegian, and Dutch Clasen or Classen , or of any other patronymic from a shortened form of the personal name Nik(o)laus or Nikla(a)s (see Nicholas ).

nameMeanings.name: Olivier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: II
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Olivier II de Clisson was born in 1236, in Clisson, Loire-Atlantique, Pays de la Loire, France, his father, Olivier Ier de Clisson, was 31 and his mother, Plaisou de la Roche-Derrien, was 34. He married Jeanne Marie Bertrand de Bricquebec about 1263. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1298, at the age of 62.

Il a épousé Jeanne Marie Bertrand de Bricquebec environ 1263. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1298, à l’âge de 62 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Olivier II de Clisson(이)가 1236년 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 로이레-앳랜티크 클리슨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Olivier Ier de Clisson(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Plaisou de la Roche-Derrien(은)는 34세였다. 그는 대략 1263년 Jeanne Marie Bertrand de Bricquebec(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1298년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.
